title: agrostemma pyrenaicum
intro: Agrostemma pyrenaicum, also known as the Pyrenean corn-cockle, is a flowering plant species native to the Pyrenees mountain range. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant is rich in flavonoids and other bioactive compounds that may provide health benefits, including antioxidant and antimicrobial activities. In herbal preparations, Agrostemma pyrenaicum is often used as a dried herb or tincture to treat various conditions, and it can also be used as a tea or infusion to promote relaxation and soothe respiratory issues.
